jokes.text: My stomach's a little more sensitive lately, so if you wouldn't mind wearing a little less perfume... And if your lunch is especially pungent, maybe have it in the break room.
jokes.setup: Pam makes what seems like a reasonable workplace request about smells
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she's being overly sensitive due to pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Sets up the pregnancy subplot with character-appropriate passive-aggressive politeness

jokes.text: Pam's pregnancy morning sickness sensitivity demands from coworkers (no perfume, different soap, eat lunch in break room)
jokes.setup: Pregnant woman making reasonable requests for morning sickness
jokes.punchline: The escalating specificity and control over office environment
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven comedy that sets up pregnancy subplot with realistic workplace awkwardness

jokes.text: A three-ounce fetus is calling the shots. It's so badass.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining Pam's pregnancy symptoms
jokes.punchline: Describing a tiny fetus as 'badass' for controlling an adult
jokes.explanation: Unexpected word choice ('badass') for describing a fetus creates humor through incongruity

jokes.text: I guess I missed the meeting where we all voted for you to get pregnant.
jokes.setup: Pam asking Phyllis to change her soap
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's sarcastic response about voting on pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phyllis passive-aggression with workplace meeting terminology

jokes.text: All morning I look forward to my afternoon cigars, and I am not stopping for anybody.
jokes.setup: Employee complaints about pregnancy accommodations
jokes.punchline: Creed planning to smoke cigars in the office
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed obliviousness to workplace norms and health concerns

jokes.text: Watching people get sick always makes me sick. And... Well, frankly, so does talking about it, so...
jokes.setup: Discussing Pam's morning sickness
jokes.punchline: Ryan getting sick talking about getting sick from watching sickness
jokes.explanation: Ryan's squeamishness and self-awareness create mild recursion humor

jokes.text: Jim showing off something he spent all morning doing, Pam's confused reaction about doing it to 'our--'
jokes.setup: Jim proudly showing off morning's work
jokes.punchline: Pam's interrupted confusion suggests he did something to their shared property
jokes.explanation: Subtle relationship comedy, works better in context of their dynamic

jokes.text: Uh, believe it or not, Kevin, firecrackers are in the 'don't' column.
jokes.setup: Wedding planning discussion
jokes.punchline: Kevin wanted to bring firecrackers to a wedding
jokes.explanation: Kevin's lack of social awareness about appropriate wedding activities

jokes.text: So you're gonna provide them, then? - No, this is a firecracker-free wedding. - What the hell? - You've got to be kidding me.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining no firecrackers
jokes.punchline: Multiple employees outraged by lack of explosives at wedding
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of multiple people expecting fireworks at wedding

jokes.text: Decent people everywhere will get offended.
jokes.setup: Discussing not mentioning pregnancy at wedding
jokes.punchline: Angela's judgmental overstatement about pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela self-righteousness and moral superiority

jokes.text: Some of us have to be our own grandmother.
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning her grandmother
jokes.punchline: Angela's bizarre statement about being her own grandmother
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Angela logic delivered with complete seriousness

jokes.text: You know, Angela, um, you don't have to come to the wedding. - Really, Pam? - Yes, she does.
jokes.setup: Pam offering Angela an out
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately contradicting his bride-to-be
jokes.explanation: Jim's quick contradiction shows his mischievous side

jokes.text: What happens in Niagara stays in Niagara.
jokes.setup: Going to Niagara Falls for wedding
jokes.punchline: Misapplying Vegas catchphrase to family-friendly tourist destination
jokes.explanation: Lazy joke stealing Vegas phrase for wrong location

jokes.text: So I will see you up here in 'Viagra' falls.
jokes.setup: Michael saying goodbye before Niagara trip
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Niagara as Viagra
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate wordplay with sexual reference

jokes.text: Pam and Jim's wedding will be the single best pickup destination in the history of the universe.
jokes.setup: Dwight preparing for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Viewing wedding as pickup opportunity with universe-level hyperbole
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inappropriate focus and ridiculous hyperbole

jokes.text: Dwight's elaborate wedding guest research including mountain bike selling history
jokes.setup: Dwight researching potential hookups
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous irrelevant details from online searches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight obsessiveness applied to dating. The mountain bike detail is brilliantly specific and useless.

jokes.text: I stole the guest list from Jim's desk, and I search-engined every female on both sides of the family.
jokes.setup: Dwight's wedding pickup plan
jokes.punchline: Stealing guest list and researching all women online
jokes.explanation: Dwight's creepy thoroughness and 'search-engined' terminology

jokes.text: Okay. You're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Dwight's terrible research on women
jokes.punchline: Andy's blunt assessment of Dwight's dating intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in dismissing Dwight's absurd dating prep

jokes.text: It's the hair.
jokes.setup: Michael planning to upgrade his appearance for wedding
jokes.punchline: Revealing his new hairstyle as the solution
jokes.explanation: Michael's misguided confidence in bad styling choices

jokes.text: Kevin's new haircut reveal and immediate regret
jokes.setup: Kevin thinking hair will make him sharper dresser
jokes.punchline: His obvious bad haircut and immediate exit
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy that works through Kevin's self-awareness of the disaster

jokes.text: Kevin's hair transformation talking head: 'It's the hair.'
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his plan to be a sharp dresser at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His solution being an apparently ridiculous hairpiece/style
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy setup for Kevin's wedding makeover attempt

jokes.text: Nah, this is so much cooler. We feel like we're in a limo and you're our driver.
jokes.setup: Michael asking someone to sit up front with him
jokes.punchline: Ryan treating Michael like hired help instead of friend
jokes.explanation: Ryan's casual cruelty toward Michael's loneliness

jokes.text: She smells like my mom.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why Erin is sweet and cute
jokes.punchline: Attraction based on maternal scent similarity
jokes.explanation: Creepy psychological insight into Michael's attractions

jokes.text: Click. Oh, you blinked. Damn it. Now that's in my brain forever. What a lousy picture. Should have hired a professional to take the mental pictures.
jokes.setup: Erin mentioning taking mental pictures at wedding
jokes.punchline: Michael treating mental pictures like real photography with complaints
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of metaphor into literal complaint about professional services

jokes.text: Mental picture joke sequence with Jim blinking
jokes.setup: Pam's aunt suggesting taking mental pictures
jokes.punchline: Jim 'ruining' the mental photo by blinking, hiring professional mental photographers
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist extension of concept. Jim and Pam's playful relationship, building on simple premise to absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: Hello. My name is Dwight Schrute. If you're listening to this, you're a lucky woman Michael has seduced.
jokes.setup: Dwight made Michael a romantic CD
jokes.punchline: Opening with formal introduction on seduction tape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight misunderstanding of romance and seduction

jokes.text: Don't be scared of your night in heaven.
jokes.setup: Dwight's romantic CD continues
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of sex as 'night in heaven'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's purple prose approach to romance instruction

jokes.text: Hey. Got the room the night before you guys. I'll break in the bed.
jokes.setup: Stranger at hotel overhearing about honeymoon suite
jokes.punchline: Offering to 'break in' their wedding bed
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable stranger interaction with sexual implication

jokes.text: I don't like that.
jokes.setup: Stranger offering to break in their bed
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated negative reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim understatement to creepy situation

jokes.text: Their proximity to the falls makes everything smell like a basement.
jokes.setup: Describing the hotel near Niagara Falls
jokes.punchline: Romantic destination smells like basement
jokes.explanation: Deflating romantic expectations with basement smell

jokes.text: And I had spoken to Theresa about a room with two safes.
jokes.setup: Dwight checking into hotel
jokes.punchline: Special request for two safes in room
jokes.explanation: Dwight's paranoid security needs for hotel stay

jokes.text: If I have a woman up there and things are getting amorous, she's gonna get turned off if she looks in the bathroom and she sees you brushing your teeth with butter and clay, okay?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why Michael can't stay
jokes.punchline: Bizarre description of Michael's hygiene routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and gross image of alternative tooth brushing

jokes.text: When Mary was denied a room at the inn, Jesus was born. When Michael was denied a room at the inn, we don't know what happens, because that story hasn't been told yet.
jokes.setup: Michael being denied hotel room
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Virgin Mary with messianic delusions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael grandiosity comparing minor inconvenience to Biblical events

jokes.text: A queen-size bed is five feet wide. I am not five feet wide, Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting sharing Stanley's bed
jokes.punchline: Stanley's logical explanation with subtle self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Stanley's matter-of-fact rejection with understated body awareness

jokes.text: I'm not a physics major, Stanley. I'm just saying be careful.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining bed width
jokes.punchline: Michael's nonsensical response about being careful
jokes.explanation: Michael missing the point and giving weird safety advice

jokes.text: You are going to be sleeping by yourself for the rest of your life, so you should just get used to it.
jokes.setup: Michael asking to stay with Kelly and Ryan
jokes.punchline: Kelly's brutal prediction about Michael's romantic future
jokes.explanation: Kelly's casually cruel assessment delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: She's the only 80-year-old with no smile wrinkles.
jokes.setup: Pointing out Meemaw at wedding
jokes.punchline: Describing grandmother's perpetual sternness through wrinkles
jokes.explanation: Clever way to communicate personality through physical observation

jokes.text: Didn't move my lips.
jokes.setup: Everyone hearing his inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Michael's futile attempt at plausible deniability
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish attempt to deny what everyone heard

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't care to live if I thought that.
jokes.setup: Pam saying nobody's perfect
jokes.punchline: Meemaw's extreme response to imperfection concept
jokes.explanation: Meemaw's dramatic overstatement reveals extreme worldview

jokes.text: Because while I was busy trying to improve the company and make it a success, Jim, the bad man, was busy kissing the boss man's butt.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Jim is his boss
jokes.punchline: Dwight calling Jim 'the bad man' while admitting political failure
jokes.explanation: Dwight's bitter explanation with childish name-calling

jokes.text: Penny mistaking Kevin for Pam's boyfriend, Kevin's outraged reaction
jokes.setup: Sister meeting Pam's coworkers
jokes.punchline: Assuming Kevin is the boyfriend, Kevin's horrified response
jokes.explanation: Good mistaken identity moment, Kevin's vanity wounded by the suggestion

jokes.text: You thought I was dating this? What the hell is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Woman thinking Kevin was Oscar's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Kevin's insulted reaction to gay assumption
jokes.explanation: Kevin's homophobic offense at mistaken assumption

jokes.text: I would be proud to date you.
jokes.setup: Kevin's offensive reaction to gay assumption
jokes.punchline: Oscar's dignified response to Kevin's rudeness
jokes.explanation: Oscar's classy response contrasts with Kevin's crudeness

jokes.text: Like I did with Erin.
jokes.setup: Discussing how Meredith got to young people's table
jokes.punchline: Revealing she switched place cards with young person
jokes.explanation: Meredith's shameless admission of wedding crasher behavior

jokes.text: In 1996, her 10th grade volleyball team went 10 and 2. What am I supposed to do with that, Dwight? - That's a very good record.
jokes.setup: Dwight sharing research on potential date
jokes.punchline: Useless sports statistic from decades ago
jokes.explanation: Dwight's completely irrelevant research and defensive response

jokes.text: Never married... Never... for a noogie.
jokes.setup: Michael giving dating advice about being sweet and kind
jokes.punchline: Advocating physical harassment as flirtation
jokes.explanation: Michael's terrible dating advice about physical harassment

jokes.text: Michael's smart car routine falling completely flat
jokes.setup: Michael doing uninvited comedy routine
jokes.punchline: Terrible material getting no laughs, bombing completely
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy routine, works as character moment showing Michael's delusions but the material itself is deliberately weak

jokes.text: Pam, I can now admit in front of friends and family that I do know how to make a photocopy. Didn't need your help that many times.
jokes.setup: Jim's wedding toast about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Admitting he faked needing help to spend time with Pam
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback revealing Jim's romantic deception throughout series

jokes.text: She shouldn't, because she's an alcoholic. Pam is an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Jim panicking about pregnancy slip
jokes.punchline: Making up worse lie about alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Jim's panic creating worse problem than original secret

jokes.text: They had an accident, and you know what? These two people, they're living together. They are having lots of consensual sex.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to 'help' explain pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of their sex life to elderly relatives
jokes.explanation: Michael making everything infinitely worse with TMI

jokes.text: And you know what? You can't expect them to be careful every time, because, frankly, it's just a different sensation.
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate sex discussion continues
jokes.punchline: Detailed explanation of unprotected sex sensations
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness at worst possible moment

jokes.text: The hotel's television set had a movie on called Bruno last night. The remote control had so darn many buttons on it, I couldn't turn it off. So I had to just sit there while it happened to me.
jokes.setup: Meemaw explaining her moral outrage
jokes.punchline: Accidentally watching inappropriate gay comedy movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Meemaw's moral shock from accidental viewing

jokes.text: Is there something about being a manager that makes you say stupid things? - I have not found that to be the case.
jokes.setup: Pam asking about management and stupid comments
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate contradiction proving her point
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - Jim proves Pam's point immediately

jokes.text: Michael's excitement about free hotel room from Meemaw leaving
jokes.setup: Learning Meemaw is leaving early due to moral outrage
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate focus on getting her room
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael selfishness, missing emotional gravity of situation for personal benefit

jokes.text: Oh, you're that foul man that kept talking about intercourse.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to talk to Meemaw
jokes.punchline: Meemaw's direct assessment of Michael's behavior
jokes.explanation: Meemaw's blunt terminology for Michael's earlier behavior

jokes.text: And I know in your day she would be considered a whore, but now women go out and they have sex and they get wild and they take their tops off and they have pictures taken of them.
jokes.setup: Michael's progressive women's rights speech
jokes.punchline: Describing modern feminism as topless photography
jokes.explanation: Michael's completely wrong understanding of women's liberation

jokes.text: They're gonna call it Meemaw. - You mean Sylvia. - Yes. And if it's a boy, they will call it Sylvio.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming baby will be named after Meemaw
jokes.punchline: Not knowing her actual name, making up masculine version
jokes.explanation: Michael making up names because he doesn't know Meemaw's real name

jokes.text: 'Cause that would help me deliver on some promises I made. Whoo!
jokes.setup: Andy asking people to bring hot women
jokes.punchline: Admitting he already promised women would be there
jokes.explanation: Andy's party planning already falling apart before it starts

jokes.text: Looks like you're going to a fish fry, Dwight. - No, they're howling at the moon. It's suggestive to women. Because of howling during sex.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Dwight's outfit
jokes.punchline: Dwight's completely wrong understanding of sexy clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic connecting wolves to sexual prowess

jokes.text: You have that thick, beautiful, Chicano hair. So nice.
jokes.setup: Toby discussing hair loss with Oscar
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate ethnic compliment about Oscar's hair
jokes.explanation: Toby's awkward ethnic observation about Oscar's appearance

jokes.text: I invented a device called Burger on the Go. It allows you to obtain six regular-sized hamburgers or 12 sliders from a horse without killing the animal.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his inventions
jokes.punchline: Extracting hamburgers from living horses
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd invention that makes no logical sense but sounds official

jokes.text: Sears said no.
jokes.setup: Listing companies considering his horse burger device
jokes.punchline: Only one company definitively rejected the absurd product
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery suggesting others are still considering it

jokes.text: They're men, Dwight. - I love finding a good set of twins.
jokes.setup: Dwight excited about twins he found
jokes.punchline: They're male twins, not women
jokes.explanation: Dwight's non-sexual fascination with twins as phenomenon

jokes.text: Meredith's robot dancing critique leading to Andy's dance battle
jokes.setup: Wedding dance floor scene
jokes.punchline: Escalating dance competition leading to Andy's injury
jokes.explanation: Sets up Andy's injury, physical comedy that works better visually

jokes.text: Did someone change my name to Baskin Robbins? Because I feel like a banana split.
jokes.setup: Dance battle escalating
jokes.punchline: Andy's terrible ice cream pun about being split
jokes.explanation: Andy's forced pun leading to his injury

jokes.text: I was dancing and I did a split and I landed on my car keys in my pocket. I tore my scrotum.
jokes.setup: Andy's dance battle bravado
jokes.punchline: Serious injury to genitals from showing off
jokes.explanation: Painful physical comedy consequence of Andy's ego

jokes.text: That Pam? Hey! Have her come out. Have her come out! It'll be like Coyote Ugly.
jokes.setup: Michael drunk and hearing Pam on phone
jokes.punchline: Wanting pregnant bride to recreate bar dancing movie
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate drunk demands at worst timing

jokes.text: 'Jim, hey, why don't you braid my hair? I want to watch TV.' Now you sound like Kermit.
jokes.setup: Michael's drunk impression of married life
jokes.punchline: Shifting to Kermit the Frog voice randomly
jokes.explanation: Michael's drunk rambling taking bizarre Muppet turn

jokes.text: When the bag was opened by our shoeshine, the smell overcame him. I, too, smelled them and made the choice that they must be thrown away. Incinerated, actually.
jokes.setup: Kevin's shoes were professionally cleaned
jokes.punchline: They were so smelly they had to be destroyed for safety
jokes.explanation: Escalating from cleaning to incineration shows Kevin's hygiene level

jokes.text: Kevin's shoes being incinerated due to smell
jokes.setup: Kevin's shoes sent for cleaning overnight
jokes.punchline: Hotel destroying them as safety hazard due to overwhelming smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin hygiene joke escalated to absurd extreme. Hotel worker's professional explanation of the destruction is excellent.

jokes.text: She's a dental hygienist from Carbondale, and she makes love like one. She's a bumpkin. Pass.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Dwight date Isabel seriously
jokes.punchline: Dwight's complete 180 from tiger comparison to dismissive rejection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction of Dwight's earlier bragging when reality sets in

jokes.text: Andy's scrotum injury pillow offer from Jim
jokes.setup: Jim being considerate of Andy's injury
jokes.punchline: Awkward discussion of cushioning Andy's damaged genitals
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to injury joke, Jim's awkward attempt at kindness

jokes.text: I have painted a portrait of the two of them from memory. And I have another one of them in the nude. But that one is for me.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining his wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Admitting to painting nude portraits of couple for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed creepiness delivered as if completely normal

jokes.text: Mom, you're totally projecting and being a drag.
jokes.setup: Pam's mom expressing wedding day doubts
jokes.punchline: Calling out her mother's behavior in therapy speak
jokes.explanation: Pam using psychology terminology to shut down mom's negativity

jokes.text: Pam's veil tearing and Jim ripping his to match
jokes.setup: Pam upset about torn wedding veil
jokes.punchline: Jim destroying his formal wear to make them match
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment showing Jim's devotion. Physical comedy with emotional weight, perfect Jim and Pam relationship beat.

jokes.text: Apricot. Made of real apes.
jokes.setup: Angela eating apricot snack
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding fruit etymology with apes
jokes.explanation: Angela's confident delivery of completely wrong word origin

jokes.text: End up going to sleep by the vending machine. It was loud, but it was warm.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his bad weekend
jokes.punchline: Sleeping by vending machine like homeless person
jokes.explanation: Michael's pathetic accommodation situation described matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I got six numbers. One more would have been a complete telephone number.
jokes.setup: Kevin talking about wedding success
jokes.punchline: Not understanding that he got individual digits, not phone numbers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic failure delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: My Kleenex shoes were a huge conversation piece, but man, my dogs are barking. My feet were so sweaty, I can't even feel the cold.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his wedding experience
jokes.punchline: Made shoes out of tissues and they're completely soaked
jokes.explanation: Kevin's creative solution to destroyed shoes backfiring spectacularly
